topic: North Magnetic Pole deviation >18° from 120°E longitude
change_summary: detail_verdict_length grew from 205 to 1508 (+635.6%); sourced from §1f in-prompt scan after drift-audit head (WIN-050, WIN-028, WIN-009) hit OBE-skip cap (3 consecutive). audit fresh (17.7h old, 2026-05-05T20:52Z) but its top 3 were re-reviewed by previous curmudgeon runs in the last day; in-prompt scan picks WIN-036 as next legitimate large_rewrite candidate (635.6% drift, no review newer than c2 2026-04-07T23:30Z). New content is the standard OTS-canary post-hoc paragraph + Timestamp Error / Part 3.1b cross-references.
our_argument_summary: Verdict 'Std Model Explains' — non-discriminating. The dome extrapolates the same NOAA NP.xy trajectory as WMM2025; WMM2025 (released Dec 2024) published the pole at ~139.3°E over 14 months before PROS-006 was registered (2026-03-06). 19.298° − 18° = 1.298°: the threshold was already exceeded by the data when registered. WIN-036 is logically downstream of WIN-007 (same NOAA pole position, restated as 'deviation from 120°E'). c3 adds the standard OTS-canary post-hoc paragraph (the 'trivial-transcription test') and cross-refs to the Timestamp Error tab and Part 3.1b post-hoc standard.

relevant_files: raw-text/03-predictions.txt:39-41 — PROS-006 registered 2026-03-06, W010 mapping; 'NOAA/BGS WMM2025 confirms pole at 139.298 deg E — deviation of +19.3 deg from 120 deg E'
build-scripts/generate-html.js:1816 — Timestamp Error tab heading id='timestamp-error'
build-scripts/generate-html.js:1820-1850 — Timestamp Error tab text describing what status_history.json contains
data/sections.json — part3 (anchor 'posthoc-standard' / id 'p3-31b' is present and confirms the 3.1b heading rendered correctly)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: monitor.py and pull_data.py are not in raw-text/ this cycle, so the previous review's claim that 'Domain #11 (NMP Longitude) fetches NOAA NP.xy and checks lon > 130°E' is taken on the c2 reviewer's word; not independently re-verified this cycle. The site's static text (raw-text/02-wins.txt and raw-text/03-predictions.txt) and the W010→WIN-036 mapping are independently re-verified.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Per c2: hardcoded — no code dynamically computes 'deviation from 120°E' or tests against the 18° threshold. The lon > 130°E live test (if real) serves WIN-007's position, not WIN-036's specific threshold. c3 keeps this characterization; cannot independently re-verify without monitor.py in raw-text/.
data_sources_used: NOAA/BGS WMM2025 (manual confirmation source, Dec 2024 release — verified against raw-text/03-predictions.txt line 41)
NOAA NP.xy time-series (referenced for WIN-007 trajectory; not independently verified in raw-text/ this cycle)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None identified for WIN-036 specifically. The 18° threshold was already exceeded at registration time; the prediction is binary (>18° from 120°E) and was registered six days before being marked 'confirmed'.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None against verifiable raw-text/. The c2 review's monitor.py Domain #11 claim could not be independently re-checked because monitor.py is not in raw-text/ this cycle. Recommend the analyst confirm Domain #11's actual comparison logic on the next pull_data refresh.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: WIN-036 (W010) confirmation is a static line in raw-text/02-wins.txt ('139.298 deg E = +19.3 deg') and raw-text/03-predictions.txt ('NOAA/BGS WMM2025 confirms pole at 139.298 deg E'). No monitor.py domain dedicated to the >18°/120°E test; per c2, Domain #11's lon > 130°E test serves WIN-007 not WIN-036. Confirmation is a manual transcription of WMM2025's published pole position into a static entry. Tag unchanged from c2.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: WMM2025 (NOAA/BGS, Dec 2024) publishes the NMP at ~139.3°E. The 19.3° figure relative to 120°E is the same pole position re-expressed in a different coordinate. The dome relabels the cause as 'aetheric-vortex threshold crossing' instead of core-mantle flux-lobe dynamics (Livermore et al. 2020, Nat. Geosci., DOI 10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9 — cited in WIN-007's detail_evidence). The dome predicts no different value than WMM2025. Tag unchanged from c2.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: WMM2025 published 139.3°E in Dec 2024 (raw-text/03-predictions.txt line 41 confirms the position; the December-2024 release date matches NOAA's standard 5-year WMM cycle following WMM2020). PROS-006 registered 2026-03-06 — over 14 months later. Confirmation 6 days after registration. The 18° threshold was already exceeded by 1.298° (139.298 − 120 = 19.298) at registration time. Tag unchanged from c2; the c3 detail_verdict_text now also explicitly invokes the Part 3.1b post-hoc standard via anchor #posthoc-standard.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: 120°E is a geographic reference meridian; 18° is a chosen threshold. Neither is derived from disc_radius=20,015 km or firmament_height=9,086 km. The dome site frames the prediction as 'consistent with the post-1990 exponential trajectory' (raw-text/03-predictions.txt line 40) — i.e., NOAA-data extrapolation, not dome-equation derivation. Tag unchanged from c2.

holes_found.description: Cross-WIN OTS-canary inconsistency. WIN-036 c3 detail_verdict_text says "OTS timestamps status_history.json, which bundles prediction parameters together with observation results in a single file. The hash proves when the bundled file existed — not that the prediction preceded the observation." The Timestamp Error tab (build-scripts/generate-html.js around line 1820, rendered at docs/index.html:3450) says the OPPOSITE: "OpenTimestamps anchors status_history.json, the file containing reference data: observed values, pass/fail results, and statistical comparisons. These are observations — the outcomes. The prediction parameters — the formulas, expected values, and tolerances — live in monitor.py source code and docs/model.html." The same boilerplate appears verbatim in WIN-035, WIN-037, WIN-038, and WIN-039 detail_verdict_text fields. Either status_history.json bundles predictions+observations (per WINs 35–39) or contains observations only with predictions in monitor.py/model.html (per Timestamp Error tab) — a defender reading both can credibly accuse us of incoherent characterization of the OTS attack surface.
holes_found.recommendation: Audit the actual contents of https://john09289.github.io/predictions/api/current/status_history.json and harmonize the language. Likely truth: status_history.json contains entries with prediction labels (descriptive text + threshold) PLUS observation results — i.e., it bundles 'prediction labels' but not 'prediction logic' (which lives in monitor.py). If so, both formulations are partially right but talk past each other; rewrite the WIN canary boilerplate to: "OTS timestamps status_history.json, which contains both the prediction labels and the observation results bundled together. The structural prediction logic — formulas and threshold computation — lives in mutable monitor.py source. The OTS hash proves only that the bundled file existed at the timestamp; it does not prove the prediction text preceded the observation it is paired with." Mirror this language in the Timestamp Error tab. (Alternative: if status_history.json really is observations-only, then the WIN-035–039 canary boilerplate is factually wrong and a defender will catch it; correct the boilerplate to match the Timestamp Error tab.) Either way, both pages must agree on what the file contains.

holes_found.description: c2 finding NOT remediated: WIN-007 still has code_analysis.relabels_standard=false while its own finding says "Standard geophysics explains the 1990s acceleration via flux lobe dynamics; dome provides only a label with no derivation or predictive content" and detail_evidence says "standard geophysics provides the complete causal mechanism". WIN-036 (same underlying NOAA NP.xy data, same pole) has relabels_standard=true. Identical phenomenon, opposite tag. The c2 review of WIN-036 (cycle 2, 2026-04-07T23:30Z) flagged this exact inconsistency as moderate; ~30 days later the tag is unchanged and no issue was opened. A defender can compile our two own statements and say 'pick one: either standard physics explains it or it doesn't.' Aggregate Part 2b counts (e.g. '14/31 relabel standard physics') may be off by one as a result.
holes_found.recommendation: Open an issue against WIN-007: change code_analysis.relabels_standard from false to true. WIN-007's evidence text already justifies the change. Re-confirm Part 2b's 'relabels_standard count' and any prose stat that depends on it. (This is a tag-data fix, not a verdict change — WIN-007 verdict 'Misleading' is unaffected.)

holes_found.description: c2 finding NOT remediated (detail_claim framing). detail_claim still says 'Magnetic pole deviation >18° from 120°E longitude is unprecedented and predictive.' The dome's actual PROS-006 wording (raw-text/03-predictions.txt line 40) is: 'The North Magnetic Pole would be more than 18 deg east of the 120 deg E meridian, consistent with the post-1990 exponential trajectory.' Our 'unprecedented and predictive' phrasing is editorial gloss; the dome actually frames it as trajectory-confirmation. Small strawman risk.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace detail_claim with the dome's actual wording or a close paraphrase: 'Magnetic pole deviation exceeds 18° east of the 120°E meridian, confirming the post-1990 exponential trajectory.' Lets the rebuttal address what the dome actually claims.

holes_found.description: c2 finding NOT remediated (batch members not named in detail_evidence). detail_evidence says PROS-006 was registered '6 March 2026 ... part of a batch of five predictions all registered the same day' but does not name WIN-035 through WIN-039 explicitly. raw-text/03-predictions.txt has the W009/W010/W011/W013/W015 mapping (one of the batched predictions, W013, maps to WIN-038 — verifiable from raw-text/03-predictions.txt line 70). A reader cannot follow the batch claim without cross-referencing.
holes_found.recommendation: Name the batch in-line: 'part of a batch of five predictions (WIN-035, WIN-036, WIN-037, WIN-038, WIN-039 / W009, W010, W011, W013, W015) all registered on 6 March 2026 and confirmed within six days.'

holes_found.description: Quantitative imprecision. detail_verdict_text says "once the pole reaches ~139°E (as WIN-007's trajectory predicts), being 19.3° from 120°E is pure arithmetic." 19.3 is correct to one decimal place, but the 19.298° value is what's actually in the dome's confirmation text (raw-text/02-wins.txt line 124, raw-text/03-predictions.txt line 41 and 67). The 1.298° margin above the 18° threshold is what makes the post-hoc nature unmistakable — using 19.3° rounds away the rhetorical force. Minor, but if the goal is to highlight that the threshold was already exceeded, the margin should be exact.
holes_found.recommendation: Use 19.298° (not 19.3°) and add the explicit margin: 'the dome's own confirmation cites 139.298°E, i.e., 19.298° from 120°E — exceeding the registered 18° threshold by 1.298° at the time of registration.' This sharpens the post-hoc point without softening the language elsewhere.

stronger_arguments.description: Explicit 'pre-registered margin' framing. The dome registered an 18° threshold against a value that was already 19.298° — a margin of 1.298° already in the bag. A genuine forecast registers a threshold the data has not yet crossed. Calling out the exact margin of safety the threshold leaves makes the post-hoc nature inarguable. Currently we say 'WMM2025 published the pole at 139.3°E months earlier' but we don't compute the headroom the dome built into its threshold relative to that pre-existing value.
sources: raw-text/03-predictions.txt:41 — '139.298 deg E — deviation of +19.3 deg from 120 deg E. Exceeds threshold.'
Arithmetic: 139.298 − 120 = 19.298; 19.298 − 18 = 1.298° margin already in the bag at PROS-006 registration.

stronger_arguments.description: Comparable trajectory equivalence. Both WMM2025's secular-variation forecast and the dome's 'post-1990 exponential trajectory' are fits to the same NOAA NP.xy time series. Asserting equivalence requires showing they make the same prediction at future epochs (e.g., what does the dome's exponential say the pole will be in 2030, vs WMM2025's secular-variation forecast for 2030?). Currently we assert non-discrimination; making this quantitative would close the 'but our trajectory is independent of WMM' defense (Lens A best defense, rated 3/5 below).
sources: WMM2025 epoch 2025–2030: pole position is published to ~0.01° precision year-by-year through 2030.
Dome's published exponential parameters (would need pull_data.py / monitor.py to extract — not in raw-text/ this cycle).

kernel_of_truth.description: The NMP's post-1990 acceleration is real, scientifically significant, and required NOAA to issue an emergency out-of-cycle WMM update in 2019 (raw-text reference for the acceleration framing is in WIN-007's detail_evidence; the underlying paper is Livermore et al. 2020, DOI 10.1038/s41561-020-0570-9, cited there). Tracking and recording threshold crossings on this trajectory reflects genuine engagement with NOAA's NP.xy time series, not random number-picking. The dome author chose a real, monitored phenomenon and registered a threshold. (data/wins.json field 'detail_evidence' for WIN-036 directly relies on the WIN-007 chain; raw-text/03-predictions.txt:39-41 confirms the registration text.)
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Recognizing a published, already-occurred phenomenon is not predicting it. The 18° threshold was exceeded by 1.298° at registration time per data/wins.json detail_evidence (139.298 − 120 − 18); WMM2025 (NOAA/BGS, Dec 2024 release per raw-text/03-predictions.txt:41) had already published 139.298°E over 14 months before the dome registered the threshold. WIN-036 adds zero numerical content beyond WIN-007's pole position — once a pole position is published, every angular distance from every reference meridian is an arithmetic restatement. The 120°E reference and 18° threshold are not derivable from dome geometry (data/wins.json code_analysis.derives_from_dome=false; reasoning recorded in code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence above). The kernel is real attention to a real phenomenon; the WIN status is the post-hoc relabel of that already-public phenomenon.

deeper_analysis:
c3 review confirms the verdict 'Std Model Explains' holds with high confidence (0.93). The change that triggered this review (detail_verdict_length 205→1508, +635.6%) added the standard OTS-canary post-hoc paragraph plus cross-refs to the Timestamp Error tab and Part 3.1b post-hoc standard — content that is structurally appropriate and rhetorically valuable.

But the review surfaces three problems the c2 round either missed or that have stagnated:

1. The new OTS-canary boilerplate (now also in WIN-035, WIN-037, WIN-038, WIN-039) characterizes status_history.json as 'bundles prediction parameters together with observation results in a single file.' The Timestamp Error tab characterizes it as observations-only with predictions in monitor.py/model.html. These are factually inconsistent. A defender pasting both into an AI gets a free 'your two pages disagree' rebuttal. This is the c3 round's biggest finding and is rated MAJOR.

2. The c2 cross-WIN tag mismatch (WIN-007 relabels_standard=false vs WIN-036 relabels_standard=true) is unfixed 30 days later. The fix is mechanical (flip WIN-007's tag).

3. Two minor c2 framing recommendations (detail_claim 'unprecedented and predictive' vs the dome's actual 'consistent with the post-1990 exponential trajectory'; batch members not named) are also unfixed. These are cheap to apply and would tighten the WIN.

The quantitative spot-check confirms 139.298 − 120 = 19.298 and 19.298 > 18. Using 19.3° throughout the WIN's prose loses the rhetorically powerful 1.298° margin that demonstrates the threshold was already exceeded by the registration data; recommend keeping 19.298° in at least one place where the post-hoc point is being made.

Dome-defender Lens A best defense (rating 3/5): 'Our exponential-trajectory model was fit to NOAA NP.xy data and committed to source before WMM2025 was published. The 18° threshold is structurally bound to that trajectory, not copied from WMM2025. OTS at minimum proves the threshold parameter existed at registration.' Refutation in our text would be tightest if we add a single sentence: 'Both NOAA's WMM2025 and the dome's purported exponential trajectory are fits to the same public NOAA NP.xy time series; they have not been shown to make distinguishable predictions at any future epoch.' This neutralizes the 'our trajectory is independent' move without strawmanning.

Verdict: 'Std Model Explains' is correct. recommended_action: minor_edit (tightenings + cross-WIN fix). recommended_action is NOT verdict_change — the verdict text and evidence are mutually consistent. The major hole is at the rhetorical-coherence layer (cross-WIN OTS-canary phrasing), not at the verdict layer.

advocate_mode.best_defense: Three-pronged dome defense, framed by a steel-manning advocate: (1) The dome's exponential trajectory model was fit to NOAA NP.xy data and committed to its source repository before WMM2025 was published. The 18° threshold is structurally bound to that trajectory model — not copied from WMM2025's published 139.3°E. The OTS chain, even granting the review's separate critique that it timestamps the wrong file, at minimum proves that the threshold parameter existed at registration time as a numerical commitment of the dome's trajectory model, not the WMM2025 readout. (2) The review's post-hoc test ('not plausibly known to the author at write-time') applies an unusually strict standard. By that standard, every confirmed prediction in physics that uses publicly known measurements would fail. The dome registered a specific quantitative threshold that was not entailed by any single prior measurement; reaching the threshold required the trajectory to continue. (3) The review's two own pages disagree about what status_history.json contains: WIN-035 through WIN-039 say it 'bundles prediction parameters together with observation results,' but the Timestamp Error tab says it contains observations only with predictions in monitor.py and docs/model.html. A defender can credibly say: pick one — if predictions are in monitor.py, then the WIN-035–039 OTS critique is mischaracterizing the OTS attack surface; if predictions are in status_history.json, then the Timestamp Error tab is misleading.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Two specific text changes preempt all three prongs: (a) Add one sentence to WIN-036 detail_evidence: 'Both NOAA's WMM2025 secular-variation forecast and the dome's purported exponential trajectory are fits to the same public NOAA NP.xy time series; they have not been shown to make distinguishable predictions at any future epoch.' This neutralizes prong 1 by treating the dome's trajectory as a duplicate of NOAA's, not a competitor. (b) Harmonize the OTS-canary boilerplate across WINs 035–039 with the Timestamp Error tab so a defender can't compile our two own statements into a 'pick one' attack — see major hole 1 above. Prong 2 (post-hoc-test-too-strict) is already preempted in detail_evidence by the 'no derivation connects 18° threshold or 120°E reference to dome geometry' sentence; a single-clause add would close the remaining gap: '...and the threshold was already exceeded by 1.298° in the data the dome registered the prediction against.'

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: TWO cross-WIN inconsistencies. (1) WIN-007 code_analysis.relabels_standard=false vs WIN-036 code_analysis.relabels_standard=true, despite both describing the same NMP position from the same NOAA NP.xy data and despite WIN-007's own evidence text ('standard geophysics provides the complete causal mechanism') being a textbook relabels_standard=true case. Flagged in c2 (2026-04-07); unchanged ~30 days later. (2) The standard OTS-canary boilerplate inserted into WIN-035, WIN-036, WIN-037, WIN-038, and WIN-039 detail_verdict_text describes status_history.json as bundling 'prediction parameters together with observation results in a single file,' while the Timestamp Error tab (build-scripts/generate-html.js around line 1820) describes status_history.json as containing 'observed values, pass/fail audit results, statistical comparisons' only — with predictions in monitor.py and docs/model.html. The two characterizations are not reconcilable as written; one of them needs to change.

claims_checked: WIN-036 detail_verdict_text rounds 19.298° to 19.3° throughout — minor rhetorical weakness; 19.298° is the exact dome-confirmation value per raw-text/02-wins.txt:124 and raw-text/03-predictions.txt:41 and :67
Timeline check: WMM2025 release Dec 2024 (per raw-text/03-predictions.txt:41) vs PROS-006 registration 2026-03-06 — gap is 14–15 months, consistent with WIN's 'over 14 months' claim
Confirmation gap: 2026-03-06 (registration) → ~2026-03-12 (confirmation per WIN-035 entry which is in the same batch) → 6 days, consistent with detail_evidence implication
quantitative_verification.computations: Arithmetic and date subtraction performed in python and node. All claims in WIN-036's detail_evidence and detail_verdict_text are arithmetically correct. The only quantitative weakness is rhetorical — using 19.3° (rounded) instead of 19.298° (exact) softens the margin-already-in-the-bag point.
